_comment: Entra ID Conditional Access Baseline Policies
_usage: These are TEMPLATES. Replace placeholder IDs before applying via Microsoft Graph API.

_replace.BREAK_GLASS_GROUP_ID: Object ID of break-glass exclusion group
_replace.CORPORATE_NAMED_LOCATION_ID: ID from namedLocations API after creating your trusted IP range

policies._comment: Policy 1: Require MFA for all users
policies._nist: IA-2 (Identification and Authentication — multi-factor for all access)
policies._mitre: T1078 (Valid Accounts) — MFA defeats credential-based attacks
policies._rationale: Single-factor authentication is insufficient. Microsoft reports 99.9% of compromised accounts lacked MFA. This policy makes MFA mandatory for all cloud app access.

users._comment: Include all users, exclude break-glass accounts to prevent lockout

applications._comment: All cloud apps — scope to specific apps if All causes friction initially

clientAppTypes: _comment: Modern auth clients only — legacy auth is blocked by separate policy

policies._nist: AC-17 (Remote Access — restrict legacy protocols), IA-2 (authentication — block bypass paths)
policies._mitre: T1110.003 (Password Spraying) — legacy auth is the primary spray target because MFA does not apply
policies._rationale: IMAP, SMTP, POP3, and basic HTTP auth do not support MFA challenges. Any org enforcing MFA without blocking legacy auth has a hole attackers exploit routinely. Block legacy auth BEFORE enabling MFA enforcement.

policies._comment: Policy 3: Sign-in risk — require MFA for medium+ risk sign-ins
policies._nist: IA-5 (Authenticator Management — respond to authenticator compromise indicators)
policies._mitre: T1078.004 (Cloud Accounts) — risk-based MFA catches ATO attempts even with valid credentials
policies._rationale: Microsoft Entra ID Protection assigns risk scores to sign-ins based on threat intelligence (leaked credentials, anonymous IP, impossible travel). Requiring step-up MFA for medium+ risk sign-ins catches account takeover attempts in progress.
policies._prerequisite: Requires Entra ID Premium P2 license for Identity Protection risk-based CA
policies.displayName: REQUIRE: MFA for Medium+ Risk Sign-ins

sessionControls._comment: Force sign-in frequency reset on risky sessions — do not allow persistent sessions

_apply_instructions: 1. Create break-glass group: az ad group create --display-name CA-Exclusion-BreakGlass --mail-nickname ca-exclusion-breakglass
2. Get group ID: az ad group show --group CA-Exclusion-BreakGlass --query id -o tsv
4. Apply Policy 2 (Block Legacy) first — no report-only needed, safe to enable immediately
5. Apply Policy 1 and 3 in report-only mode — monitor sign-in logs for 1-2 weeks
6. Move to enabled: az rest --method PATCH --url 'https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/identity/conditionalAccess/policies/<id>' --body '{"state": "enabled"}'
